title: garnotia courtallensis
intro: Garnotia courtallensis, also known as Courtallensis or Wild Jasmine, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Ghats in India. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of Garnotia courtallensis include infusions, decoctions, and essential oil extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
